name: MongoDB (Sharded)
description: Document-oriented NoSQL database with native sharding for horizontal scaling and flexible schema design

license.comment: Server Side Public License since October 2018; source-available but not OSI-approved

hosting-options.comment: Self-hosted Community/Enterprise, MongoDB Atlas (official managed), serverless Atlas option, and third-party providers like Aiven

initial-release.comment: First released in February 2009 by 10gen (now MongoDB Inc.)

data-model.comment: BSON document model with flexible schema; supports nested documents and arrays

topology.comment: Each shard is a replica set with primary-secondary topology; mongos routers distribute queries

sharding.comment: Supports hashed sharding, range-based sharding, and zone sharding; shard key must be chosen manually

replication-factor.comment: Default 3-node replica sets per shard; configurable from 1 to 50 members

multi-region.comment: Zone sharding enables geo-distributed deployments; Atlas provides multi-region clusters

cap-position.comment: Tunable via read/write concerns; defaults to CP with primary reads, configurable for AP

consistency-model.comment: Configurable per operation: linearizable (with snapshot), causal sessions, read-your-writes, eventual consistency

acid-transactions.comment: Full multi-document ACID transactions since v4.0 (replica sets) and v4.2 (sharded clusters)

transaction-scope.comment: Distributed transactions span multiple shards, collections, and databases within a cluster

isolation-levels.comment: Transactions use snapshot isolation by default; read concern 'majority' provides read-committed

distributed-transactions.comment: Cross-shard transactions supported since v4.2; some limitations on creating collections in cross-shard transactions

query-language.comment: MongoDB Query Language (MQL) with JSON-like syntax; aggregation pipeline for complex operations

sql-compatibility.comment: No native SQL support; uses MQL and aggregation framework. Third-party tools like Atlas SQL Interface provide limited SQL

secondary-indexes.comment: Supports single-field, compound, multikey, text, geospatial, and hashed indexes

joins.comment: $lookup performs left outer joins within aggregation pipeline; recommended to denormalize for performance

aggregations.comment: Powerful aggregation pipeline with $group, $match, $sort, $project and many stages

full-text-search.comment: Text indexes for basic full-text search; Atlas Search provides advanced search with $search stage

max-cluster-size.comment: Qihoo runs 1,500+ nodes supporting 20B operations/day; Baidu runs 600 nodes with 1PB data

write-throughput.comment: Production reference: Qihoo serves 20 billion operations per day across 1,500 nodes

read-latency-p99.comment: Typical latency for indexed queries; varies significantly based on query complexity and data size

auto-scaling.comment: Atlas provides auto-scaling for compute and storage; self-hosted requires manual intervention

hot-spot-handling.comment: Balancer automatically migrates chunks between shards; chunks split when exceeding size threshold

zero-downtime-upgrades.comment: Rolling upgrades supported for replica sets and sharded clusters; upgrade one node at a time

online-schema-changes.comment: Schema-less design allows adding fields without migration; indexes can be built in rolling fashion

point-in-time-recovery.comment: Available via Ops Manager/Atlas continuous backups with oplog replay; Percona tools also support PITR

backup-strategy.comment: mongodump/mongorestore snapshots, filesystem snapshots, continuous oplog-based backups via Ops Manager/Atlas

kubernetes-operator.comment: MongoDB Controllers for Kubernetes (MCK) - official operator supporting Community and Enterprise editions

rust-driver.comment: Official MongoDB Rust driver maintained by MongoDB

java-driver.comment: Official MongoDB Java driver with sync and reactive variants

python-driver.comment: PyMongo - official Python driver with 4.3K GitHub stars

ruby-driver.comment: Official MongoDB Ruby driver; Mongoid ODM also available

c-cpp-driver.comment: Official MongoDB C and C++ drivers

orm-support.comment: Prisma supports MongoDB; Mongoose is the popular Node.js ODM (not a traditional ORM)

open-source.comment: SSPL is not OSI-approved; considered source-available rather than open source

free-tier.comment: Atlas M0 free tier with 512MB storage; no credit card required

pricing-model.comment: Atlas pricing based on instance hours, vCPU, RAM, storage, and data transfer

cost-estimate-small.comment: M10 dedicated cluster starts at ~$57/month; M2/M5 shared tiers at $9-25/month

change-data-capture.comment: Change streams provide CDC functionality built on oplog; available since v3.6

watch-subscribe.comment: watch() method allows clients to subscribe to real-time changes on collections, databases, or deployments

triggers.comment: Atlas Database Triggers execute Atlas Functions on data changes; runs on serverless compute layer

event-streaming.comment: Official MongoDB Kafka Connector for source and sink; Confluent-verified
